name: Three Lines Become Four: A Storytelling Workshop with Naveed Mulki and Pankaj Singh
description: How does a lived experience become the first lines of a story? How does a passing thought become a manuscript? Can a conversation inspire a feature film? 'Three lines become Four', led by Naveed Mulki and Pankaj Singh is an exploration of the process behind narrative building. Join us for an interactive workshop that explores storytelling across formats. Learn how to identify a story, how to approach and construct it using the tools available, how to draw inspiration and more. Here are the details of the Event:   Date:  10th May , Sunday Time:   11 AM to 1 PM Price:   INR 799 Venue:   Champaca Bookstore, Vasanth Nagar Since seats are limited, we recommend that you  sign up here ! About the Speakers:  Naveed Mulki  is a self-taught writer and filmmaker. His work involves not just finding diverse characters but deepening the way their narratives are told. His journey has intuitively gravitated toward culture, conservation, and the human experience. His current work includes an ongoing experimental film series for MAP (Museum of Art and Photography) in Bangalore, where he responds to its diverse collection; an experimental art-historical film retelling the ecological edicts of Ashoka using the ancient theatre of Koodiyattam; a feature documentary that explores Tamil and Irish fishing cultures through their stories and poetry, which revolve around community and offer a fresh perspective on climate change and unfettered capitalism; and a short documentary in Goa on the last guardians of fungal knowledge, who endure in preserving a delicate ecosystem of memory, spirituality, and survival. Pankaj Singh   is a writer and filmmaker whose work reflects his deep curiosity for the natural world. Through Faraway Originals, a collective he co-founded, he tells stories that lie at the intersection of nature, science, and human connection. His narratives are quiet, immersive, and rooted in simplicity. Limited Seats.  RSVP  at the earliest.
